items.title: Comment attachment schema is applied locally
items.precondition: The active environment points to the local appdb database.
steps: Run npx knex migrate:list.
Confirm 20260715000000_add_pm_comment_attachments.ts is completed.
items.expected: pm_task_attachments.comment_id is available without applying unrelated pending migrations.
items.evidence: Knex reported 20260715000000_add_pm_comment_attachments.ts among 192 completed migrations. The unrelated metric-action migration remained pending and was not run.

items.title: Comment composer exposes and handles the full image workflow
steps: Render the comment composer with image support.
Select an @mention and a picker image.
Exercise image-only, non-image rejection, paste, drop, preview, disabled, and saving states.
items.expected: Mention chips, image previews, paste/drop/picker input, image-only comments, validation, and send states behave as designed.
items.evidence: CommentComposer.test.tsx reported 4 tests passed, including visible affordances, mention selection, picker preview, non-image rejection, paste/drop, image-only submit, and disabled saving state.

items.title: Comment images persist under the parent comment only
items.precondition: Local API is healthy on http://localhost:3000 and the PM migration is applied.
steps: Create a temporary task and comment through the authenticated PM API.
Upload a one-pixel PNG with comment_id.
List comments and standalone attachments.
Delete the comment and temporary task.
items.expected: The image appears under the comment, never appears in the standalone attachment list, and its metadata/S3 object are removed during cleanup.
items.evidence: Live run used task 63c3c723-1a80-4112-b1c5-9bf8044aa34a, comment 545d7fdf-2a3a-4e87-8538-2b569a322547, and attachment faad29b4-6c69-4793-b4e5-b1db8095e21e. Nested response, standalone exclusion, cascade metadata removal, S3 deletion, and temporary-data cleanup all passed.

items.title: Mention email recipients are filtered and intercepted
items.precondition: Mailgun is configured and NODE_ENV=development keeps the email interceptor active.
steps: Create a comment whose mention list includes a duplicate and the actor.
Edit the comment to add one new mention.
Inspect the resulting email_logs rows.
items.expected: Only the distinct non-actor mention receives each logical email, all local sends are intercepted to Dave, and comment deep links are present.
items.evidence: Two Mailgun sends completed with status sent. Original recipients were corey@getalloro.com on create and jordan@getalloro.com for the newly-added edit mention. Both stored rows were intercepted to dave@getalloro.com and contained tab=comments deep links. QA log rows were removed after capture.

items.title: Movement email rules distinguish reorder from state change
items.precondition: A temporary task is assigned to another internal user.
steps: Move the task within its current column.
Confirm the PM email-log count does not change.
Move the task to a different column and inspect the new email log.
items.expected: Same-column reorder sends no email; cross-column movement sends one de-duplicated, intercepted details-link email.
items.evidence: Same-column email count delta was 0. Moving To Do to In Progress produced exactly one sent Mailgun row for original recipient corey@getalloro.com, intercepted to Dave, with a tab=details deep link.

items.title: Project Board deep links resolve the requested task tab
items.precondition: Frontend URL utilities and production build are available.
steps: Run projectBoardDeepLink.utils.test.ts.
Inspect captured comment and movement email HTML.
Request http://localhost:3000/admin from the running app.
items.expected: comments and details query values resolve correctly, emails include the matching tab, and the authoritative admin SPA is served.
items.evidence: The deep-link utility reported 2 tests passed; captured email HTML contained task plus tab=comments/details; /admin returned the Vite SPA through the local API proxy.

items.title: Regression and architecture gates pass
items.precondition: All implementation and acceptance changes are present.
steps: Run backend and frontend test suites.
Run backend and frontend production builds.
Run strict conventions, lint, dependency checks, TypeScript, and git diff --check.
items.expected: No blocking errors remain; baseline advisory warnings are documented.
items.evidence: Backend: 68 files and 500 tests passed. Frontend full suite: 47 files and 167 tests passed; the final focused set reported 12 tests passed. Both builds, strict conventions, check:all, TypeScript, scoped lint with zero errors, dependency cruise with zero errors, and diff checks passed.
items.notes: Baseline lint and dependency warnings remain advisory; no new blocking Constitution violation was introduced.
